howItWorks.title: How does it work?
howItWorks.description: The feedback tab displays all user ratings (thumbs up/down) on Scavenger's responses. Use it to monitor how well the system is performing and identify areas that need improvement.
howItWorks.hint: Click any feedback entry to see full details including the conversation history. Review negative feedback to spot recurring issues—then save a corrected query as an example or create rules to teach the system the correct behavior. Over time, this feedback loop helps the system deliver more accurate, reliable answers.

howItWorks.title: How does it work?
howItWorks.description: Set the ground rules for your data. Define which records should be included, how different data tables connect, and what your key metrics actually mean.
howItWorks.hint: These rules act as guardrails—ensuring everyone gets consistent, trustworthy answers that follow your company's standards. Once you've documented them, the system automatically applies them to every question, so your team never has to worry about getting conflicting or incorrect information.

delete.title: Delete Query Example

howItWorks.title: How does it work?
howItWorks.description: Show the system what good looks like. Save your team's most common questions alongside the correct answers—like "Which regions are performing best?" with its proven query.
howItWorks.hint: Each example you add teaches the system to recognize similar questions and respond instantly with results formatted exactly how your team prefers. The more examples you provide, the wider the range of questions it can handle confidently.

outstanding.label: Outstanding
outstanding.description: Your data quality is exceptional. This dataset is pristine and ready for analysis.

great.label: Great Quality
great.description: Your data quality is very good. Minor improvements could enhance analysis accuracy.

acceptable.label: Acceptable
acceptable.description: Your data quality is acceptable. Consider addressing some quality issues for better results.

needsAttention.label: Needs Attention
needsAttention.description: Your data has several quality issues that should be addressed before analysis.

tooltips.correlations: Correlations show how two columns move together, calculated using a correlation coefficient from paired values. For example, as marketing spend rises, sales typically increase, indicating a positive overall trend.
tooltips.missingValues: Missing values are empty entries, calculated by counting blanks or nulls per column. For example, if 5 out of 100 ages are missing in a survey, it indicates incomplete responses and potential bias in the analysis.
tooltips.outliers: Outliers are unusually high or low values, calculated using statistical thresholds like z-scores or IQR. For example, a $10,000 sale among $100 sales clearly stands out as a rare event in the data.
tooltips.dataQualityAlerts: Data quality alerts flag potential issues, calculated based on rules regarding missingness, duplicates, or outliers.

emptyStates.numerical: No numerical columns in this dataset
emptyStates.categorical: No categorical columns in this dataset
emptyStates.datetime: No datetime columns in this dataset

batch.title: Generate All Profiles
batch.description: Profile all {{count}} tables and views in one click.
batch.failedBanner: Failed: {{tables}}. Click retry or re-profile individually.
batch.skippedBanner: Skipped (row limit exceeded): {{tables}}. These tables are too large to profile.

suggestions.HIGH_NULL_COLUMNS: These columns have a very high percentage of null values. Consider removing them or investigating why data is missing.
suggestions.MIXED_TYPE_COLUMN: Clean the column by converting all values to the same type, or split into separate columns.
suggestions.HIGH_MISSING_DATA: Consider filling missing values with defaults, or remove the column if not critical.
suggestions.SEPARATOR_CURRENCY_DETECTED: Remove currency symbols and thousands separators before analysis.
suggestions.TRAILING_WHITESPACE: Trim leading/trailing whitespace using data cleaning tools.
suggestions.ZERO_VARIANCE: This column provides no information - consider removing it from analysis.
suggestions.HIGH_OUTLIER_PERCENTAGE: Review outliers to determine if they're errors or valid extreme values.
suggestions.DUPLICATE_ROWS: Remove duplicate rows or verify if duplicates are intentional.
suggestions.CONSTANT_COLUMNS: This column has the same value in all rows - consider removing it from analysis.

messages.HIGH_OUTLIER_PERCENTAGE: Column has unusually high percentage of outliers ({{pct}}%)
messages.ZERO_VARIANCE: Column has zero variance (single unique value)

rules.desc: Guardrails for how Scavenger writes SQL, covering business logic, calculations, and data conventions.
